# Contentkit — Full Documentation for LLMs

This single file contains the complete Contentkit documentation for LLM
consumption. A concise index is in `docs/llms.txt`; the committed OpenAPI
snapshot is in `docs/openapi.json`.

Contentkit is an API-first Markdown mini-CMS for personal websites, blogs and
project portfolios. It stores CMS metadata and immutable content revisions in a
dedicated PostgreSQL database, stores assets and release objects in private
Supabase Storage, and serves clean static websites through atomically activated
release pointers.

- Production API: your own deployment (examples use https://contentkit-api.example.com)
- Local API: http://127.0.0.1:4050
- Source: https://github.com/MikeBild/contentkit
- License: MIT

---

## 1. Core concept

Contentkit turns Markdown into a complete static website:

```
site config + Markdown + assets
  ──content API──▶ immutable content items and revisions
  ──preview/release──▶ rendered static files
  ──Supabase Storage──▶ immutable release prefix
  ──ck_activate_release()──▶ active public website
```

The public website is static output. Most pages need no JavaScript. Search,
Mermaid, contact forms, comment forms and Turnstile load only on pages that use
them. HTML is cache-revalidated; first-party assets (site.css, forms.js, …) are
content-hashed (`/assets/<name>-<hash>.<ext>`) and served `immutable`, so a new
release changes the URL and returning visitors always fetch the current build
instead of a stale cached copy. Rollback activates a known release pointer
without re-rendering or copying files.

Contentkit is not a visual page builder and not a generic Supabase application.
It is a focused, API-first Markdown-to-static-site CMS with explicit operational
boundaries.

---

## 2. Base URLs and discovery

Use these service endpoints for discovery and health checks:

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/` | JSON service descriptor on the configured API host |
| GET | `/health` | Liveness; returns `ok` while the process is up |
| GET | `/ready` | Readiness; returns status, version and in-flight release count |
| GET | `/metrics` | Prometheus metrics |
| GET | `/openapi.json` | Generated OpenAPI 3.1 spec |
| GET | `/llms.txt` | Concise LLM documentation index |
| GET | `/llms-full.txt` | Full LLM documentation in one file |

Production examples in this file use:

```text
https://contentkit-api.example.com
```

The committed OpenAPI snapshot is generated with:

```bash
npm run docs:gen-openapi
```

CI runs `npm run check:docs-drift`. That test fails when `docs/openapi.json`
differs from generated OpenAPI, when this file omits an OpenAPI path, when
`docs/llms.txt` links to missing files, or when the HTTP LLM documentation
endpoints do not serve the committed files.

---

## 3. Authentication, scopes and API keys

Management endpoints accept either:

```http
Authorization: Bearer <key>
```

or:

```http
X-API-Key: <key>
```

Production has a bootstrap key from `CONTENTKIT_BOOTSTRAP_API_KEY`. Treat it as
a break-glass credential. Routine automation should use scoped keys created via
`POST /v1/api-keys`.

### Key model

A key looks like `ck_<43-char base64url>` (a `ck_` prefix over 32 random bytes).
The server never stores the raw key: it keeps only `key_hash =
HMAC-SHA256(key = CONTENTKIT_KEY_PEPPER, message = raw key)` (hex) plus a short
`key_prefix` (the first 11 characters, e.g. `ck_A1b2C3d4`) for identification.
The raw key is returned **only once**, in the `key` field of the `POST
/v1/api-keys` response, and cannot be recovered later — store it in a secret
manager immediately. Because the hash is peppered, rotating
`CONTENTKIT_KEY_PEPPER` invalidates every existing key (they will then fail with
401, see below).

### Authentication errors: 401 vs 403

These two statuses mean different things and are fixed in different ways:

| Status | Body | Meaning | How to fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| `401` | `{"error":"unauthorized"}` | The credential was **not accepted**: no key, malformed header, revoked, expired, or the HMAC hash matched no stored key. Sent with a `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer` header. | Verify you sent the exact raw key and that `CONTENTKIT_KEY_PEPPER` matches the pepper in force when the key was created. Do **not** touch scopes — the key was never even recognized. |
| `403` | `{"error":"insufficient_scope","scope":"<required>","site":"<site-id>"}` | The key **is valid and recognized** but lacks the required scope, or is restricted to `site_ids` that do not include the target site. | Use (or mint) a key that carries the named `scope` for that `site`. |

Worked example: a valid `site:admin` key that returns `401` is not a
scope/authorization problem — the key bytes or the server pepper are wrong (or
the key was revoked). By contrast, a `content:write` key calling an
admin-only endpoint returns `403 insufficient_scope` with `"scope":"site:admin"`
— that key is fine, it just isn't allowed there.

### Scopes

| Scope | Allows |
|---|---|
| `site:admin` | Create/read/update sites and create scoped API keys |
| `content:read` | List content items and revisions, read site metadata |
| `content:write` | Upload Markdown/assets and create immutable revisions |
| `release:write` | Build previews, publish releases, run scheduled publish and roll back |
| `moderation:write` | List/moderate comments and contact submissions |
| `*` | Bootstrap-only global access |

Keys can also be restricted to `site_ids`. If `site_ids` is empty, the key is
global for its scopes. If `site_ids` contains UUIDs, authorization succeeds only
for those sites.

Recommended key split:

| Key | Scopes | Use |
|---|---|---|
| Site admin key | `site:admin` | Create/read/update sites and create additional scoped keys |
| Publishing key | `content:read content:write release:write` | Upload Markdown, build previews, publish releases and roll back |
| Moderation key | `moderation:write` | Review comments and contact submissions |
| Scheduler key | `content:read content:write release:write moderation:write` if needed by workflow design | Scheduled publish automation |

A key with only `site:admin` cannot publish content. A publishing key without
`site:admin` cannot create sites or additional keys. This separation is
intentional.

Create a publishing key:

```bash
curl -X POST "$CONTENTKIT_URL/v1/api-keys" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CONTENTKIT_SITE_ADMIN_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "publisher",
    "scopes": ["content:read", "content:write", "release:write"],
    "site_ids": ["<optional-site-id>"]
  }'
```

Create a site-admin key:

```bash
curl -X POST "$CONTENTKIT_URL/v1/api-keys" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CONTENTKIT_BOOTSTRAP_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name":"site-admin","scopes":["site:admin"]}'
```

Suggested shell variables:

```bash
export CONTENTKIT_URL="https://contentkit-api.example.com"
export CONTENTKIT_SITE_ADMIN_KEY="ck_..."
export CONTENTKIT_PUBLISH_API_KEY="ck_..."
```

---

## 4. Complete HTTP API

### Management endpoints

| Method | Path | Scope | Function |
|---|---|---|---|
| POST | `/v1/sites` | `site:admin` | Create a site, locales and optional hostnames |
| GET | `/v1/sites/{site}` | `content:read` or `site:admin` | Read site metadata and settings |
| PATCH | `/v1/sites/{site}` | `site:admin` | Update site metadata, base URL, default locale and settings |
| GET | `/v1/sites/{site}/content` | `content:read` | List content items for a site |
| POST | `/v1/sites/{site}/content` | `content:write` | Create a content item if needed and create its first immutable revision |
| GET | `/v1/content/{item}/revisions` | `content:read` | List immutable revisions for one content item |
| PUT | `/v1/content/{item}/revisions` | `content:write` | Create another immutable revision for an existing content item |
| GET | `/v1/sites/{site}/published` | `content:read` | List published content as JSON (read API): `kind`, `locale`, exact `tag`, `updated_since`, keyset `limit`/`cursor` |
| GET | `/v1/sites/{site}/published/{kind}/{locale}/{slug}` | `content:read` | Read one published document as JSON incl. `markdown` source and on-demand rendered `html` |
| GET | `/v1/sites/{site}/search` | `content:read` | Full-text search over published content: required `q`, optional `locale`, `kind`, `limit`; ranked results with `<mark>` headlines |
| POST | `/v1/sites/{site}/previews` | `release:write` | Build an expiring preview release and return a preview URL |
| GET | `/v1/sites/{site}/releases` | `content:read` | List releases newest first (for audit and rollback targets) |
| POST | `/v1/sites/{site}/releases` | `release:write` | Build and atomically activate a public release |
| POST | `/v1/sites/{site}/releases/{release}/activate` | `release:write` | Roll back by activating a prior release pointer |
| DELETE | `/v1/content/{item}/published` | `release:write` | Unpublish a content item from the live site |
| GET | `/v1/content/{item}/audio` | `content:read` | Read-aloud (TTS) job status and audio asset URL for one item |
| DELETE | `/v1/content/{item}/audio` | `release:write` | Delete all audio jobs and generated MP3 assets for one item; schedules an auto-rebuild |
| POST | `/v1/sites/{site}/audio/backfill` | `release:write` | Enqueue read-aloud audio jobs for published posts (`dry_run`, `limit_chars`, `slugs`, `force`) |
| GET | `/v1/sites/{site}/audio/jobs` | `content:read` | List audio jobs (`status`, `limit`) plus per-status counters and monthly budget summary |
| POST | `/v1/publish-due` | `release:write` | Publish scheduled revisions grouped by site |
| POST | `/v1/maintenance/storage-gc` | `release:write` (unrestricted) | GC old release objects and reap stuck builds |
| GET | `/v1/comments` | `moderation:write` | List pending/approved/rejected comments, optionally filtered by site/status |
| PATCH | `/v1/comments/{comment}` | `moderation:write` | Approve or reject a comment; approval can publish a new release |
| GET | `/v1/contact-submissions` | `moderation:write` | List contact submissions, optionally filtered by site |
| PATCH | `/v1/contact-submissions/{id}` | `moderation:write` | Mark a contact submission as `read` or `closed` |
| GET | `/v1/feedback` | `moderation:write` | Per-post feedback aggregates (`up`/`down` counts), optionally filtered by `site_id`/`post` |
| POST | `/v1/api-keys` | `site:admin` | Create a scoped API key; raw key is returned once |
| GET | `/v1/sites/{site}/webhooks` | `site:admin` | List webhook endpoints (secrets never returned) |
| POST | `/v1/sites/{site}/webhooks` | `site:admin` | Register a signed webhook endpoint; `whsec_` secret returned once |
| PATCH | `/v1/sites/{site}/webhooks/{endpoint}` | `site:admin` | Update url/events/description or enable/disable an endpoint |
| DELETE | `/v1/sites/{site}/webhooks/{endpoint}` | `site:admin` | Delete a webhook endpoint |
| POST | `/v1/sites/{site}/webhooks/{endpoint}/rotate` | `site:admin` | Rotate the endpoint signing secret; new secret returned once |
| GET | `/v1/webhook-deliveries` | `site:admin` | List deliveries (status, attempts, last error) for observability |
| POST | `/v1/webhook-deliveries/{delivery}/retry` | `site:admin` | Manually re-queue a delivery |

### Public endpoints

| Method | Path | Function |
|---|---|---|
| POST | `/public/v1/contact` | Submit a contact request |
| POST | `/public/v1/posts/{post}/comments` | Submit a comment for moderation on a post |
| POST | `/public/v1/posts/{post}/feedback` | Submit a one-click feedback vote (`vote: "up"` or `"down"`); requires `settings.feedback.enabled: true` |

Public endpoints require `site_id` in the request body. They use an in-memory IP
rate limit, a honeypot field named `website`, input length checks, email
validation, and Cloudflare Turnstile in production. The feedback endpoint is
deliberately captcha-free: its payload is an enum-only anonymous vote (no
reader data is stored), so the honeypot and rate limit are its whole gate.

### Static gateway endpoints

The same service also serves published static sites:

| Route | Function |
|---|---|
| `/p/{token}/...` | Expiring preview release; `noindex`, `private,no-store` |
| `/media/{asset-id}/...` | Content-addressed uploaded asset; immutable cache |
| Custom domain paths | Active release files resolved by request host |

Extensionless public paths redirect to trailing-slash directory routes. Missing
release objects fall back to `/404.html`.

### Read API

`GET /v1/sites/{site}/published` and
`GET /v1/sites/{site}/published/{kind}/{locale}/{slug}` serve currently
published content as JSON for headless consumers (build pipelines, other
renderers). They live on the management API behind `content:read` scoped keys —
there is no anonymous delivery path, and static site delivery is unaffected.

- List entries: `{ item_id, kind, locale, translation_key, slug, title,
  summary, tags, metadata, revision_id, published_at, updated_at }` plus a
  top-level `next_cursor` (null on the last page). `metadata` is the revision's
  frontmatter contract verbatim, including author-owned `extra` fields.
- List filters: `kind`, `locale`, exact `tag` and `updated_since` (ISO 8601;
  strictly greater than the item's `updated_at`, which release activation bumps
  exactly when the published revision changes — so a poller can pass the newest
  `updated_at` it has seen). Sorted by `updated_at` descending, item id as the
  tiebreak. Pages default to 50 entries (values above 200 are clamped);
  `cursor` is opaque — pass `next_cursor` back verbatim.
- The single document adds `markdown` (the immutable revision source verbatim)
  and `html` (rendered on demand; HTML is never stored). An unknown,
  unpublished or mismatched kind/locale/slug answers
  `404 {"error":"published content not found"}`.
- Caching: the list responds with a weak ETag over the site's publish epoch,
  the single document with a strong ETag over the revision source hash plus the
  service version; both honour `If-None-Match` with `304`.

### Search

`GET /v1/sites/{site}/search?q=` runs PostgreSQL full-text search over
currently published content, behind the same `content:read` scoped keys.
Published sites keep their static client-side search (`search-index.json`);
this route is an API-host feature for headless consumers and changes nothing
about site delivery.

- Query parameters: `q` (required; websearch syntax, trimmed, 1–200
  characters), `locale`, `kind` (`page`/`post`/`project`) and `limit`
  (default 20; values above 100 are clamped). Missing or overlong `q`,
  an unknown `kind` or a non-numeric `limit` answer `422`.
- Response: `{ "query": "<q>", "results": [ { item_id, kind, locale, slug,
  title, summary, tags, rank, headline, published_at, updated_at } ] }`,
  ranked by relevance. `headline` is a snippet with `<mark>…</mark>`
  highlights around the matched terms.
- Weighting: title (A), summary and tags (B), body text (D). The frontmatter
  block is stripped before indexing, so author-owned `extra` fields never
  land in the search index. Vectors are filled by an insert trigger on the
  immutable revisions; the query joins exclusively over
  `published_revision_id`, so drafts, scheduled and archived revisions are
  invisible by construction.
- Locale-aware stemming: `de` stems with `german`, `en` with `english`,
  anything else with `simple`. Without `locale` the query is stemmed with
  `simple` against locale-stemmed vectors — cross-locale search is
  best-effort; with `locale` the stemming matches the indexed side exactly.
- No ETag or cache headers: results depend on the query text, not on a
  stored artifact a version could name.

---

## 5. Site administration

Create a site:

```bash
curl -X POST "$CONTENTKIT_URL/v1/sites" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CONTENTKIT_SITE_ADMIN_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "slug": "personal",
    "name": "Example Person",
    "description": "Personal website and blog.",
    "base_url": "https://example.com",
    "default_locale": "de",
    "locales": ["de", "en"],
    "domains": ["example.com"],
    "settings": {
      "eyebrow": "Software · AI · Products",
      "hero_title": "Hello.",
      "hero_text": "Personal website built from Markdown.",
      "accent": "#2563eb",
      "theme_color": "#f8fafc",
      "profile_image": "/media/...",
      "profile_image_alt": "Portrait",
      "turnstile_site_key": "<public-turnstile-site-key>",
      "socials": {
        "GitHub": "https://github.com/example"
      }
    }
  }'
```

Important site fields:

| Field | Function |
|---|---|
| `slug` | Optional stable slug; defaults from name when omitted |
| `name` | Site title, brand label and schema.org person name |
| `description` | SEO description and RSS channel description |
| `base_url` | Canonical public website URL used in sitemap/RSS/canonical links |
| `default_locale` | Locale used by root redirect and fallback 404 |
| `locales` | Locales generated by the static site builder |
| `domains` | Hostnames that map public gateway requests to this site. PATCH replaces the list in full (empty array removes all mappings); omit the key to leave them alone |
| `settings.eyebrow` | Small hero label |
| `settings.hero_title` | Home hero headline |
| `settings.hero_text` | Home hero copy |
| `settings.accent` | Primary/accent color. Authored as a hex (`#2563eb`); the site builder converts it to the shadcn-style `H S% L%` triple that `--primary` expects (the theme consumes it via `hsl(var(--primary))`). An HSL triple (`221 83% 53%`) is also accepted verbatim. Injecting a raw hex into `--primary` unconverted breaks every `hsl(var(--primary))` rule (e.g. an invisible submit button). |
| `settings.theme_color` | `<meta name="theme-color">` |
| `settings.profile_image` | Optional hero image/avatar URL |
| `settings.profile_image_alt` | Alt text for profile image |
| `settings.turnstile_site_key` | **Public** Turnstile site key injected into contact/comment forms. Turnstile is per-site: it is only enforced+rendered when THIS is set. If the server has a global `CONTENTKIT_TURNSTILE_SECRET` but the site has no `turnstile_site_key`, the form renders no widget, produces no token, and every submission fails `422 captcha verification failed`. Set BOTH the public site key here and the matching secret in the env — they are a Cloudflare pair. (Cloudflare test pair: site key `1x00000000000000000000AA` + secret `1x0000000000000000000000000000000AA` always pass; the widget shows a "Testing only" banner — do not use in production.) |
| `settings.socials` | Footer social links and schema.org `sameAs` |
| `settings.og_image` | Default Open Graph/Twitter image for pages without a `cover` |
| `settings.og_image_alt` | Alt text for the default OG image |
| `settings.favicon` | Favicon URL (also used for the web app manifest icon) |
| `settings.apple_touch_icon` | Apple touch icon URL |
| `settings.mask_icon` | Safari mask-icon URL |
| `settings.twitter_handle` | `@handle` for `twitter:site`/`twitter:creator` |
| `settings.analytics` | `{ "provider": "plausible", "domain": "..." }` or `{ "provider": "ga4", "id": "G-..." }` |
| `settings.ai.share_buttons` | Default on. `false` hides the AI share row on posts (Markdown link, copy button, "open in Claude/ChatGPT" deep links). The links hand the article to the *reader's own* assistant — the site never talks to an AI provider. The post's Markdown twin (`index.md`) is emitted regardless |
| `settings.ai.share_targets` | Optional replacement for the default deep-link targets: `[{ "label": "...", "url_template": "https://...?q={q}" }]`; `{q}` receives the URL-encoded localized prompt. Non-http(s) templates are dropped |
| `settings.blog.subscribe_row` | Default on. `false` hides the RSS subscribe row on the blog index (RSS link, "open in Feedly" link, copy-feed-URL button) |
| `settings.blog.subscribe_targets` | Optional replacement for the blog row's reader targets: `[{ "label": "...", "url_template": "..." }]` with `{feed}` (absolute feed URL), `{feed_encoded}` and `{feed_no_scheme}` placeholders. Templates outside http(s) and the podcast-app protocols (`podcast:`, `overcast:`, `pktc:`) are dropped |
| `settings.audio.subscribe_targets` | Same shape, for the blogcast page's subscribe row. Defaults to Apple Podcasts, Overcast and Pocket Casts deep links |
| `settings.feedback.enabled` | Default off (opt-in). `true` renders a one-click "Was this post helpful?" thumbs-up/down widget under each post, posting to `/public/v1/posts/{post}/feedback`. Votes are anonymous (no reader data), deduplicated per device via localStorage |
| `settings.theme.tokens` | Per-site design tokens injected as an inline `<style>` on every page. Allowlisted keys only (`background`, `foreground`, `muted`, `muted_foreground`, `border`, `primary`, `primary_foreground`, `radius`, `font_family`) — an unknown key fails the write with 422 so typos surface. Each key maps onto the stylesheet custom property of the same name (underscores become hyphens, e.g. `muted_foreground` → `--muted-foreground`). A value is one string for both color schemes or `{ "light": "...", "dark": "..." }` for a `prefers-color-scheme` override; color values accept hex (converted to the `H S% L%` triple like `settings.accent`) or a triple verbatim. Each value is at most 256 bytes and must not contain `<` (422 otherwise) — values are emitted verbatim into the `<style>` element, so quoted font stacks like `"Helvetica Neue", Arial, sans-serif` work. `settings.accent` remains the shorthand for the `primary` token; the explicit token wins on conflict. `font_family` additionally applies `body{font-family:var(--font-family)}`. A theme is a token assignment — there are no template or layout overrides |
| `settings.theme.custom_css` | Escape hatch for whatever tokens do not cover: a raw CSS string appended as the last `<style>` element on every page. Must be a string of at most 8192 bytes and must not contain `</style` (422 otherwise); the site owner authors it, so there is no further sanitizing |

Read a site:

```bash
curl "$CONTENTKIT_URL/v1/sites/<site-id>" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CONTENTKIT_SITE_ADMIN_KEY"
```

Update a site:

```bash
curl -X PATCH "$CONTENTKIT_URL/v1/sites/<site-id>" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CONTENTKIT_SITE_ADMIN_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"description":"Updated description","settings":{"accent":"#0f766e","eyebrow":"...","socials":{}}}'
```

`settings` is replaced in full, not merged. A `PATCH` carrying only `accent` drops
every other key — analytics, socials, the Turnstile site key. Read the site first,
merge into the object you get back, then send the whole thing. `GET` accepts either
`content:read` or `site:admin`, so the key that patches can also read.

Updating site settings affects future previews/releases. Existing active release
files remain unchanged until a new release is built.

---

## 6. Content model and frontmatter

Contentkit supports three content kinds:

| Kind | Route |
|---|---|
| `page` | `/{locale}/{slug}/` |
| `post` | `/{locale}/blog/{slug}/` |
| `project` | `/{locale}/projects/{slug}/` |

Each Markdown write creates an immutable revision. A content item is identified
by `site_id + kind + locale + translationKey`. A `PUT /v1/content/{item}/revisions`
request cannot change kind, locale or translation key for that item.

Supported YAML frontmatter:

| Field | Required | Function |
|---|---:|---|
| `kind` | no | `page`, `post` or `project`; defaults to `page` |
| `title` | yes | Title, navigation label and SEO title basis |
| `locale` | yes | IETF-ish tag such as `de`, `en` or `en-us` |
| `slug` | no | URL segment; generated from title when omitted |
| `translationKey` / `translation_key` | no | Stable key linking translations; defaults to slug |
| `summary` | no | Meta description/card copy; excerpt generated when omitted |
| `date` / `publishedAt` | no | Published date for posts/projects |
| `scheduledAt` | no | Marks revision as scheduled until `/v1/publish-due` publishes it |
| `updatedAt` | no | Used in rendered metadata when supplied |
| `tags` | no | Posts only. Drives tag pages, the tag index, per-tag feeds, related posts and `article:tag`. Tags on projects are ignored |
| `cover` (or `image`) | no | Per-page Open Graph/Twitter image; overrides `settings.og_image` |
| `coverAlt` (or `imageAlt`) | no | Alt text for the cover image |
| `noindex` | no | Emit `<meta name="robots" content="noindex,nofollow">` for this page |
| `tldr` | no | List of strings. Authored key takeaways: rendered as an open "In short" block above the prose, exported to the Markdown twin and llms-full.txt, used as the JSON-LD `abstract` and added to the search index |
| `faq` | no | List of `{ q, a }` entries. Authored FAQ: rendered as collapsed questions after the prose and emitted as `FAQPage` JSON-LD |
| `featured` | no | Sort projects ahead of non-featured projects |
| `technologies` | no | Stored in metadata for project use |
| `externalUrl` | no | Stored in metadata for project use |
| `navOrder` | no | Includes pages in the header navigation, sorted ascending. Values above 60 place the page in the footer's legal column instead |
| `extra` | no | Author-owned map of custom fields, stored verbatim in the revision metadata with YAML types preserved. Max 32 keys matching `[a-z][a-z0-9_]{0,63}` (nested map keys too); values are scalars, lists of up to 64 scalars or flat maps of up to 32 scalars; 16 KiB total. Rendered as a definition list on the page and as a bullet block in the Markdown twin and per-site llms-full.txt only when `settings.content.show_extra` is `true`; never in JSON-LD or the search index |
| `related` | no | List of same-locale post slugs (max 8, no duplicates, no self-reference), stored as `related_slugs` in the revision metadata. Authored references lead the related-posts block in the author's order; tag similarity fills up to three. A reference that resolves to no published post is dropped at build time with a warning, never a release failure |

There are no custom content kinds. A dedicated collection is `kind: post` plus
a dedicated tag plus `extra` fields — the tag page supplies the listing and its
feed for free; one-off pages are `kind: page`.

Example post:

```md
---
kind: post
title: A first post
locale: en
slug: first-post
translationKey: first-post
summary: A complete Contentkit example.
date: 2026-06-29T10:00:00+02:00
tags: [Contentkit, Markdown]
---

# A first post

Contentkit supports **GFM**, footnotes, KaTeX, Mermaid and Shiki.
```

---

## 7. Markdown support

The Markdown renderer supports:

- YAML frontmatter;
- GitHub Flavored Markdown, including tables and task-list inputs;
- footnotes;
- math with KaTeX;
- fenced code highlighting via Shiki using GitHub light/dark themes;
- safe `:::note`, `:::tip` and `:::warning` directives rendered as callouts;
- Mermaid code fences rendered client-side only on pages that contain Mermaid;
- autolinked headings with stable heading IDs.

Raw HTML is sanitized. Script URLs and arbitrary unsafe markup are not passed
through. Mermaid runs with strict initialization in the browser.

---

## 8. Asset upload and media delivery

Markdown can be uploaded as raw `text/markdown`:

```bash
curl -X POST "$CONTENTKIT_URL/v1/sites/<site-id>/content" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CONTENTKIT_PUBLISH_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: text/markdown; charset=utf-8" \
  --data-binary @post.md
```

Or as multipart with assets:

```bash
curl -X POST "$CONTENTKIT_URL/v1/sites/<site-id>/content" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CONTENTKIT_PUBLISH_API_KEY" \
  -F "document=@post.md;type=text/markdown" \
  -F "asset:images/hero.jpg=@hero.jpg;type=image/jpeg"
```

Asset field names must start with `asset:`. The remaining value is the path used
inside Markdown. Contentkit rejects empty paths and traversal segments. Uploaded
asset bytes are SHA-256 content-addressed and stored once per site in Supabase
Storage. Markdown references like `](images/hero.jpg)` and `src="images/hero.jpg"`
are rewritten to immutable `/media/<asset-id>/<filename>` URLs.

`/media/...` responses use the asset content type, an ETag based on the SHA-256
hash and immutable cache headers. Plain image and audio types are served inline;
anything else is forced to download.

### Read-aloud audio (Vorlesen)

Contentkit can pre-render a spoken MP3 for every published post. Enable it per
site via settings (merge, do not replace — `PATCH` overwrites `settings`):

```json
{ "audio": { "enabled": true, "provider": "google",
  "voice": "de-DE-Chirp3-HD-Charon", "monthly_char_budget": 950000,
  "auto_rebuild": true, "blogcast_link": true,
  "blogcast_image": "https://example.com/cover.jpg",
  "blogcast_category": "Technology" } }
```

Publishing a release enqueues one job per published post revision in
`ck_audio_jobs`; a background worker (started with `CONTENTKIT_AUDIO_ENABLED=true`)
extracts the speech text from the Markdown (frontmatter, fenced code/mermaid, the
`## Weiterführende Quellen` section, URLs and the italic series line are dropped;
headings and list items become sentences), synthesizes it with Google Chirp 3 HD
(chunked ≤3800 bytes, LINEAR16 24 kHz, encoded to 64 kbps mono MP3 via the host's
`ffmpeg`), and files the MP3 as a normal content-addressed asset. Idempotency is
`UNIQUE(item_id, speech_sha256)` over the *extracted speech text*, so editing a
code block or the sources section never re-synthesizes. A post opts out with
frontmatter `audio: false`. The next release renders a player above the post
(native `<audio preload="none">` fallback plus a custom control bar:
play/pause, ±15 s, seek slider, tempo switch, remembered position, a small
`download` link) and, when at least one post has audio, a blogcast feed at
`/{locale}/blogcast.xml` with `<enclosure>`, `itunes:author`, optional
`itunes:image`/`itunes:category` and `<language>` from the locale — plus a
human-facing blogcast page at `/{locale}/blogcast/` (cover, channel
title/description, a subscribe row — RSS link, podcast-app deep links, a
copy-feed-URL button — one card per episode with the same player; indexable,
in the sitemap). The layout advertises the blogcast feed with
a `<link rel="alternate">` and a footer link to the blogcast page only when
`settings.audio.blogcast_link` is `true`; the page itself exists regardless.
The deprecated pre-1.8 `podcast_link`/`podcast_image`/`podcast_category`
spellings are still read as fallbacks; explicit `blogcast_*` keys win.

Lifecycle and operations (see `docs/audio.md` for the full guide):

- Auto-rebuild: a finished job schedules a debounced release per site (empty
  `revision_ids`, reason `audio auto-rebuild`,
  `CONTENTKIT_AUDIO_REBUILD_DEBOUNCE_MS`, default 60 s) so new narrations go
  live without a manual publish; opt out via `settings.audio.auto_rebuild:
  false`. No loop: the enqueue hook only fires for releases with revisions.
- Budget: auto-enqueue refuses a job that would push the current UTC month's
  characters (all non-skipped jobs) past `monthly_char_budget` and logs
  `audio budget exhausted`; backfill keeps its own explicit `limit_chars`.
- `GET /v1/content/{item}/audio` reports job status;
  `DELETE /v1/content/{item}/audio` removes the item's jobs and generated MP3
  assets (storage object + asset row) and schedules an auto-rebuild, returning
  `{item_id, deleted_jobs, deleted_assets, rebuild_scheduled}`.
- `POST /v1/sites/{site}/audio/backfill` fills the archive newest-first within
  a character budget (`dry_run: true` estimates cost without enqueuing;
  `slugs` narrows to specific posts; `force: true` re-renders unchanged text
  and deletes the superseded MP3 at the swap point).
- `GET /v1/sites/{site}/audio/jobs?status=&limit=` lists jobs newest-first
  (limit default 100, max 500; invalid status is 422) with a `summary` of
  per-status counters, `chars_this_month`, `monthly_char_budget` and
  `budget_remaining`.

---

## 9. Static output

For each configured locale, Contentkit generates:

| Output | Path |
|---|---|
| Locale home | `/{locale}/` |
| Blog listing | `/{locale}/blog/` (feed: newest 12 posts, topic chips, link to the archive) |
| Project listing | `/{locale}/projects/` |
| Archive | `/{locale}/archive/` (full index: every post, grouped by year, client-side tag + text filter) |
| Search page | `/{locale}/search/` (noindex; the header search form's GET target, prefills from `?q=`) |
| Contact page | `/{locale}/contact/` |
| Search index | `/{locale}/search-index.json` |
| RSS feed | `/{locale}/feed.xml` |
| Blogcast feed | `/{locale}/blogcast.xml` (only when `settings.audio.enabled` and at least one post has audio) |
| Blogcast page | `/{locale}/blogcast/` (same gate as the blogcast feed; episode list with players, in the sitemap) |
| Tag index | `/{locale}/tags/` |
| Tag pages | `/{locale}/tags/{tag}/` (`noindex,follow` and unlisted when the tag has a single post) |
| Tag feeds | `/{locale}/tags/{tag}/feed.xml` (only for tags with two or more posts) |
| LLM index | `/{locale}/llms.txt` (llmstxt.org format, describing the site) |
| LLM full text | `/{locale}/llms-full.txt` (every published document's Markdown source) |
| Post Markdown twin | `/{locale}/blog/{slug}/index.md` (the post as raw Markdown — title, canonical URL, TL;DR, body; not generated for `noindex` posts) |
| Content pages | by kind-specific route |

Global generated files:

| Output | Path |
|---|---|
| Root redirect | `/index.html` redirects to `/{default_locale}/` |
| LLM index | `/llms.txt` — a copy of the default locale's |
| LLM full text | `/llms-full.txt` — a copy of the default locale's |
| Sitemap | `/sitemap.xml` |
| Robots | `/robots.txt` |
| Not found page | `/404.html` |
| Web app manifest | `/manifest.webmanifest` |
| CSS/JS/fonts | `/assets/...` |

One deployment serves the admin API and every published site on different
hostnames. Contentkit's own surface — `/`, `/openapi.json`, `/llms.txt`,
`/llms-full.txt` and `/metrics` — answers only when the request `Host` matches the
hostname of `CONTENTKIT_PUBLIC_URL`. On a site domain those paths fall through to
the gateway, so `/llms.txt` there is the *site's* llms.txt, generated into the
release, never this documentation. `/health` and `/ready` remain reachable on every
host because probes address them by IP.

Generated metadata includes canonical links, alternate hreflang links for
translations (with `x-default`), full OpenGraph (`og:site_name`, `og:locale`,
`og:image` from the page `cover` or `settings.og_image`, and `article:*` on
posts), Twitter cards, favicons + web app manifest, optional privacy-first
analytics (Plausible or GA4), RSS discovery links and JSON-LD for the home page
(`Person`) and posts (`BlogPosting`).

The layout includes:

- accessible skip link and navigation;
- header navigation entries from pages with `navOrder` up to 60, plus the
  built-in blog/archive/project links;
- a header search field: a `role="combobox"` input with a live results dropdown,
  responsive down to a full-width second header row on narrow screens;
- footer contact and legal links (pages with `navOrder` above 60) and copyright;
- social links from `settings.socials`;
- optional Turnstile script only when forms are present and a site key exists;
- optional Mermaid scripts only when a page contains Mermaid;
- `search.js` on every page; it fetches `/{locale}/search-index.json` lazily on
  the first interaction with the search field, so a page view costs no extra
  request;
- `audio.js` and `audio.css` only on pages that render a read-aloud player;
  the script adds the tempo switch (1×/1.25×/1.5×) and remembers the listening
  position per audio URL in localStorage;
- `archive.js` on the archive page only; it filters the server-rendered list in
  place by tag and free text, fetching nothing. Every post is rendered server
  side, and the facet chips are real links to the tag pages, so the archive stays
  a complete, crawlable index without scripting. Only the free-text field, which
  has no server-side counterpart, is hidden until the script runs.

Post pages additionally carry an estimated reading time (prose only — fenced code,
math and image alt text are excluded), tag pills, related posts (cosine similarity
over IDF-weighted tag vectors, so a tag carried by every post contributes nothing),
older/newer navigation labelled by chronology, and — for posts older than three
years — a notice that the content may be out of date. Setting `updatedAt` in the
frontmatter suppresses that notice for another three years.

---

## 10. Preview, release and rollback

Build a preview:

```bash
curl -X POST "$CONTENTKIT_URL/v1/sites/<site-id>/previews" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CONTENTKIT_PUBLISH_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"revision_ids":["<revision-id>"],"expires_in":3600,"reason":"review"}'
```

Preview details:

- overlays selected revisions on top of the currently published snapshot;
- creates a release row with kind `preview`;
- uploads complete static output under an immutable release prefix;
- returns a URL under `/p/{token}/`;
- stores only a hash of the preview token;
- caps expiration at seven days internally;
- serves preview HTML with `X-Robots-Tag: noindex,nofollow,noarchive` and
  `private,no-store`.

Publish a release:

```bash
curl -X POST "$CONTENTKIT_URL/v1/sites/<site-id>/releases" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CONTENTKIT_PUBLISH_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"revision_ids":["<revision-id>"],"reason":"initial release"}'
```

Release details:

- renders the full site snapshot: `revision_ids` are overlaid on top of every
  currently published item, and `retire_item_ids` are removed from it — items
  in neither list keep their published revision unchanged;
- uploads every object before activation;
- records a manifest in `ck_release_entries`;
- marks the release `ready`;
- calls `ck_activate_release()` to switch the active site pointer and published
  revision state in one database transaction;
- leaves the previous public site active if render or upload fails;
- emits `contentkit.release.failed` to the outbox on failure.

Unpublish (retract) content:

```bash
# remove one item from the live site
curl -X DELETE "$CONTENTKIT_URL/v1/content/<item-id>/published" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CONTENTKIT_PUBLISH_API_KEY"

# or retire several items in one release, optionally publishing others
curl -X POST "$CONTENTKIT_URL/v1/sites/<site-id>/releases" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CONTENTKIT_PUBLISH_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"retire_item_ids":["<item-id>"],"reason":"unpublish"}'
```

Unpublishing archives the item's published revision, clears its published
pointer and activates a fresh release without it. Revisions stay immutable —
republishing any revision of the item brings it back. `DELETE .../published`
returns 409 if the item is not currently published.

List releases and roll back:

```bash
curl "$CONTENTKIT_URL/v1/sites/<site-id>/releases" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CONTENTKIT_PUBLISH_API_KEY"

curl -X POST "$CONTENTKIT_URL/v1/sites/<site-id>/releases/<release-id>/activate" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CONTENTKIT_PUBLISH_API_KEY"
```

Rollback activates an existing `ready`, `active` or `superseded` release. It
does not rebuild or copy files, and it does not rewrite per-item published
revision pointers — the next regular release is built from those pointers.

Storage lifecycle: each release uploads a full immutable snapshot, so old
snapshots accumulate. `POST /v1/maintenance/storage-gc` (cron-triggered, an
unrestricted `release:write` key) sweeps them: it deletes the objects and rows
of releases that are past `CONTENTKIT_RELEASE_RETENTION_MS`, not currently
active, outside the per-site `CONTENTKIT_RELEASE_HISTORY_KEEP` rollback window,
and not referenced by a live preview token; it also reaps releases stuck in
`building` past `CONTENTKIT_BUILDING_REAP_MS`. A failed or concurrency-aborted
build also removes its own partial uploads immediately. The active release and
recent rollback targets are never deleted.

---

## 11. Scheduled publishing

A revision with `scheduledAt` is stored with status `scheduled`. It is not
published until a release includes it.

`POST /v1/publish-due` finds scheduled revisions whose `scheduled_at` is due,
groups them by site and publishes one release per site. If multiple due
revisions exist for the same content item, only the latest is published and the
earlier due revisions are archived in the same run, so published content never
walks backwards over successive runs. A failure for one site is isolated and
reported per-site; it does not abort publishing for the other sites.
Unpublishing an item also archives its pending scheduled revisions, so a
retracted post cannot be resurrected by a later publish-due run.

Typical automation is any external scheduler (cron, CI schedule or a workflow engine) calling this endpoint every minute:

```text
POST /v1/publish-due
Authorization: Bearer <key with release:write>
```

Use an idempotency key in the workflow layer; Contentkit itself makes releases
atomic but does not expose an idempotency header on this endpoint.

---

## 12. Public forms, comments and moderation

Generated contact pages submit to:

```text
POST /public/v1/contact
```

Body fields:

| Field | Required | Notes |
|---|---:|---|
| `site_id` | yes | Site UUID |
| `name` | yes | Max 80 chars |
| `email` | yes | Max 254 chars, basic email validation |
| `message` | yes | Max 10000 chars |
| `website` | no | Honeypot; if filled, request is accepted but ignored |
| `cf-turnstile-response` / `turnstile_token` | production | Turnstile token |

Generated post comment forms submit to:

```text
POST /public/v1/posts/{post}/comments
```

Body fields:

| Field | Required | Notes |
|---|---:|---|
| `site_id` | yes | Site UUID |
| `name` | yes | Max 80 chars |
| `email` | no | Max 254 chars, basic email validation |
| `message` | yes | Max 5000 chars |
| `website` | no | Honeypot |
| `cf-turnstile-response` / `turnstile_token` | production | Turnstile token |

Submitted comments start as `pending`. Approve or reject:

```bash
curl -X PATCH "$CONTENTKIT_URL/v1/comments/<comment-id>" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CONTENTKIT_MODERATION_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"status":"approved","publish":true}'
```

If a comment is approved and `publish` is not `false`, Contentkit publishes a
new release so the approved comment appears on the static post page.

Contact submissions can be listed and marked:

```bash
curl "$CONTENTKIT_URL/v1/contact-submissions?site_id=<site-id>" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CONTENTKIT_MODERATION_API_KEY"

curl -X PATCH "$CONTENTKIT_URL/v1/contact-submissions/<id>" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CONTENTKIT_MODERATION_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"status":"read"}'
```

Valid contact statuses are `new`, `read` and `closed`. Valid comment statuses
are `pending`, `approved` and `rejected`.

When `settings.feedback.enabled` is `true`, the generated post pages render a
one-click "Was this post helpful?" widget that submits to:

```text
POST /public/v1/posts/{post}/feedback
```

Body fields:

| Field | Required | Notes |
|---|---:|---|
| `site_id` | yes | Site UUID |
| `vote` | yes | `up` or `down` — nothing else is accepted |
| `website` | no | Honeypot |

No Turnstile token: the vote is anonymous and enum-only, so the honeypot and
the per-IP rate limit are the whole gate. Votes are not moderated — read the
per-post aggregates instead:

```bash
curl "$CONTENTKIT_URL/v1/feedback?site_id=<site-id>" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CONTENTKIT_MODERATION_API_KEY"
```

---

## 13. Webhooks and signed notifications

Contentkit delivers events (especially the user-submitted contact and comment
forms) to configurable per-site endpoints as Standard Webhooks signed messages.

### Endpoints (subscriptions)

Register endpoints per site with `POST /v1/sites/{site}/webhooks`
(`site:admin`). Each endpoint has its own `whsec_` signing secret (returned
once on create and on `/rotate`, stored encrypted at rest) and an optional
`events` filter (empty = all events; entries match the full type, a bare suffix
like `contact.submitted`, or `contentkit.<entry>`). Endpoint URLs must be https
and are SSRF-validated on registration — private, loopback, link-local and
cloud-metadata targets are rejected. An event fans out to every enabled
matching endpoint. The legacy `CONTENTKIT_WEBHOOK_URL` /
`CONTENTKIT_WEBHOOK_SECRET` still receives all events as an implicit
built-in endpoint when configured.

Events emitted:

| Event type | Cause | `data` fields |
|---|---|---|
| `contentkit.contact.submitted` | A contact form submission was accepted | `name`, `email`, `message` |
| `contentkit.comment.submitted` | A comment was submitted for moderation | `post_id`, `author_name`, `author_email`, `body`, `status` |
| `contentkit.comment.approved` | A comment was approved | `post_id`, `author_name`, `body` |
| `contentkit.release.failed` | A release/preview build failed | — |
| `contentkit.content.published` | An item's published revision changed during release activation (a no-op republish emits nothing) | `item_id`, `kind`, `locale`, `translation_key`, `slug`, `title`, `revision_id`, `release_id` |
| `contentkit.content.unpublished` | A published item was retired by a release | same as `content.published`; `revision_id` is the retired revision |
| `contentkit.release.published` | A release was activated (also for rollbacks and empty releases, which move no item pointers and emit no `content.*` events) | `release_id`, `reason`, `published_count`, `unpublished_count` |

Content events are enqueued in the same database transaction as the
`ck_activate_release()` pointer switch and carry no absolute URLs (the URL
layout is the site builder's contract).

### Payload and signing

The JSON body carries the full submitted content:

```json
{
  "event_id": "<uuid>",
  "type": "contentkit.contact.submitted",
  "site": { "id": "<uuid>", "name": "Example" },
  "occurred_at": "2026-07-02T12:00:00.000Z",
  "data": { "name": "Ada", "email": "ada@example.com", "message": "Hello" },
  "resource": { "kind": "contact", "id": "<uuid>" },
  "summary": "New contact request"
}
```

Delivery headers follow Standard Webhooks:

| Header | Value |
|---|---|
| `webhook-id` | Unique delivery id (a UUID). Dedupe on this — a retried delivery keeps the same id. |
| `webhook-timestamp` | Unix seconds when the attempt was signed. Reject if outside your tolerance window. |
| `webhook-type` | The event type, e.g. `contentkit.contact.submitted`. |
| `webhook-signature` | `v1,<signature>` where `<signature>` is `base64(HMAC-SHA256(secret, "{webhook-id}.{webhook-timestamp}.{raw-body}"))`. Contentkit rotates secrets atomically (a single active secret per endpoint), so it emits exactly one `v1,...` value — but per the Standard Webhooks spec a robust consumer should still tolerate multiple space-separated `v1,...` values and accept if any matches. |

To verify a delivery: take the **raw request body bytes** (do not re-serialize
the JSON — key order and whitespace must match), build the signed content
`` `${webhookId}.${webhookTimestamp}.${rawBody}` ``, HMAC-SHA256 it with the
endpoint secret, base64-encode, and compare in constant time against each
`v1,`-prefixed value in `webhook-signature`. Then check `webhook-timestamp`
freshness and dedupe by `webhook-id`.

Important — the HMAC key is the **entire secret string including the `whsec_`
prefix**, taken as raw UTF-8 bytes. Contentkit does **not** base64-decode the
part after `whsec_`, so off-the-shelf Standard Webhooks / Svix verifiers (which
strip `whsec_` and base64-decode the remainder) will compute a different
signature and reject valid deliveries. Use the secret verbatim:

```js
import { createHmac, timingSafeEqual } from 'node:crypto'

function verify(secret, headers, rawBody, toleranceSeconds = 300) {
  const id = headers['webhook-id']
  const timestamp = headers['webhook-timestamp']
  if (Math.abs(Date.now() / 1000 - Number(timestamp)) > toleranceSeconds) return false
  // `secret` is the full "whsec_..." string, used verbatim as the HMAC key.
  const expected = createHmac('sha256', secret).update(`${id}.${timestamp}.${rawBody}`).digest('base64')
  const expectedBuf = Buffer.from(expected)
  return headers['webhook-signature'].split(' ').some((part) => {
    const sig = part.startsWith('v1,') ? part.slice(3) : null
    const sigBuf = sig ? Buffer.from(sig) : null
    return sigBuf && sigBuf.length === expectedBuf.length && timingSafeEqual(sigBuf, expectedBuf)
  })
}
```

### Delivery guarantees

Events are enqueued in the same database transaction as the form write (no lost
events). Delivery is at-least-once with a per-request timeout, exponential
backoff with jitter, and up to `CONTENTKIT_WEBHOOK_MAX_ATTEMPTS` attempts before
a delivery is marked `failed`. An endpoint that accumulates
`CONTENTKIT_WEBHOOK_CIRCUIT_THRESHOLD` fully-failed deliveries is auto-disabled
(re-enable via `PATCH .../webhooks/{endpoint}` with `{"enabled":true}`). Inspect
and replay deliveries with `GET /v1/webhook-deliveries` and
`POST /v1/webhook-deliveries/{delivery}/retry`.

Recommended automation:

1. a scheduled job (cron or similar) that calls
   `POST /v1/publish-due`.
2. a webhook receiver that validates signed
   events and forwards selected notifications.

Always verify the Standard Webhooks signature at the receiver; never accept unsigned deliveries.

---

## 14. Local development

Requirements: Node 20.12+ and Docker Desktop.

```bash
npm install
npm start
```

`npm start` is zero-config for local development. It loads `.env.defaults`,
starts or reuses PostgreSQL 16 in Docker, starts a persistent local
Storage/Webhook boundary and serves Contentkit at `127.0.0.1:4050`.

Local state:

| State | Location |
|---|---|
| PostgreSQL | Docker volume `contentkit-local-postgres` |
| Local storage/webhook boundary | `.contentkit-local/` |

Reset local state:

```bash
npm run local:reset
```

Create and publish a local personal profile demo:

```bash
npm run demo:profile
```

Then open:

```text
http://127.0.0.1:4050/de/
```

For a server without the local helper, use:

```bash
npm run serve
```

To migrate without starting HTTP:

```bash
node server.mjs --migrate
```

---

## 15. Configuration

Production config is explicit. Missing required production values fail startup.

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `NODE_ENV=production` | disables `.env.defaults` and enables strict required config |
| `HOST` | bind address |
| `PORT` | HTTP port |
| `CONTENTKIT_PUBLIC_URL` | canonical API/public base URL |
| `DATABASE_URL` | dedicated Contentkit PostgreSQL database URL |
| `CONTENTKIT_BOOTSTRAP_API_KEY` | initial break-glass admin key |
| `CONTENTKIT_KEY_PEPPER` | server-side API key hashing pepper |
| `CONTENTKIT_PREVIEW_SECRET` | preview token hashing secret |
| `SUPABASE_URL` | Supabase project URL for Storage API |
| `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` | service-role key for private Storage bucket |
| `CONTENTKIT_STORAGE_BUCKET` | Storage bucket name; default `contentkit` |
| `CONTENTKIT_WEBHOOK_URL` | legacy built-in webhook endpoint URL (receives all events) |
| `CONTENTKIT_WEBHOOK_SECRET` | Standard Webhooks signing secret for the built-in endpoint |
| `CONTENTKIT_TURNSTILE_SECRET` | Cloudflare Turnstile secret |
| `CONTENTKIT_TRUST_PROXY` | trust `X-Forwarded-For` behind a reverse proxy |
| `CONTENTKIT_MAX_BODY_BYTES` | upload body limit |
| `CONTENTKIT_BUILD_CONCURRENCY` | concurrent release builds |
| `CONTENTKIT_WEBHOOK_POLL_MS` | delivery worker poll interval |
| `CONTENTKIT_WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_MS` | per-delivery HTTP timeout; default 10000 |
| `CONTENTKIT_WEBHOOK_MAX_ATTEMPTS` | attempts before a delivery is marked failed; default 10 |
| `CONTENTKIT_WEBHOOK_CIRCUIT_THRESHOLD` | failed deliveries before an endpoint auto-disables; default 5 |
| `CONTENTKIT_WEBHOOK_ALLOW_PRIVATE` | allow http/private webhook targets (default true off-production) |
| `CONTENTKIT_TURNSTILE_DEV_BYPASS` | accept public submissions without a Turnstile secret; local dev only, default false (fail-closed) |
| `CONTENTKIT_RELEASE_HISTORY_KEEP` | releases kept per site as a rollback window; default 5 |
| `CONTENTKIT_RELEASE_RETENTION_MS` | minimum age before a non-active, unreferenced release is GC-eligible; default 7 days |
| `CONTENTKIT_BUILDING_REAP_MS` | age after which a release stuck in building is reaped; default 1 hour |
| `CONTENTKIT_AUDIO_ENABLED` | start the read-aloud TTS worker; default false |
| `CONTENTKIT_AUDIO_POLL_MS` | audio job poll interval; default 15000 |
| `CONTENTKIT_AUDIO_MAX_ATTEMPTS` | attempts before an audio job is marked failed; default 5 |
| `CONTENTKIT_AUDIO_REBUILD_DEBOUNCE_MS` | per-site debounce before the audio auto-rebuild release; default 60000 (min 1000, max 3600000) |
| `CONTENTKIT_FFMPEG` | path to the ffmpeg binary for MP3 encoding; default `ffmpeg` |
| `CONTENTKIT_TTS_GOOGLE_API_KEY` | Google Cloud TTS API key (sent as `?key=`) |
| `CONTENTKIT_TTS_GOOGLE_TOKEN` | OAuth bearer token alternative to the API key |
| `CONTENTKIT_TTS_GOOGLE_QUOTA_PROJECT` | `x-goog-user-project` quota project header |
| `LOG_LEVEL` | structured log verbosity |

Never reuse the Supabase service-role key as a Contentkit, database or webhook
credential.

---

## 16. Database and migrations

Contentkit owns its schema directly in a dedicated PostgreSQL database on the
existing production cluster. It does not use Supabase SQL migrations.

The binary embeds the ordered SQL migration journal and SQL bodies from
`src/db/migrations/`. On every normal boot and on `--migrate`, Contentkit opens a
dedicated PostgreSQL connection, takes a stable advisory lock, applies pending
migrations transactionally and records each journal row in the same transaction.
Stable tags are authoritative, legacy hash-only rows are adopted, and edited
historical SQL is recorded as hash drift without executing it again.

Operational implication: deployment scripts provision the database and login,
but the deployed Contentkit binary applies application SQL itself. A migration
failure aborts HTTP startup.

Add a migration:

1. create an ordered `.sql` file under `src/db/migrations/`;
2. add the matching journal entry under `src/db/migrations/meta/_journal.json`;
3. run `npm run db:gen-embedded`;
4. run migration tests before release.

---

## 17. Binary-only deployment

Contentkit is binary-only for distribution. `package.json` exists for local
development and build metadata, is marked `private`, and does not define an npm
package binary or npm publish file list.

Build:

```bash
npm ci
npm run build:binary
```

The resulting `dist/contentkit` embeds Node, dependencies, source, assets,
documentation, development defaults and the ordered SQL migration journal. It
extracts a content-hashed runtime under `$HOME/.cache/contentkit/<hash>` on
first start.

GitHub release tags matching `package.json` build:

- `contentkit-linux-x64`
- `contentkit-linux-arm64`
- `contentkit-macos-arm64`
- `SHA256SUMS`

A typical deployment downloads the Linux x64
binary, ships it to the production host, applies embedded migrations under the
service account and performs a smoke test against `/ready`, `/health`,
`/openapi.json` and an unauthorized write check.

---

## 18. Security model

Security boundaries:

- CMS metadata lives in the dedicated `contentkit` PostgreSQL database.
- The Storage bucket is private; Contentkit alone has the service-role key.
- API keys are HMAC-hashed with a server-side pepper and scoped to sites/actions.
- Preview tokens are random, hashed, expiring and revocable.
- Markdown raw HTML is sanitized; unsafe links/scripts do not pass through.
- Mermaid runs in strict mode.
- Public writes pass Turnstile, honeypot, length checks and IP rate limits.
- Webhook delivery uses signed Standard Webhooks messages and an outbox worker.
- Release activation is transactional; failed renders/uploads do not alter the
  active public site.

Treat all Contentkit management keys as production credentials. Store them in a
secret manager and prefer scoped keys over the bootstrap key.

---

## 19. Verification and troubleshooting

Run local checks:

```bash
npm run lint
npm test
npm run check:docs-drift
npm run check:embedded-drift
npm run test:integration
npm run test:e2e:local
npm audit
```

Common failures:

- startup fails with missing config: production mode requires explicit env vars;
- startup fails during migrations: embedded migrations could not be applied, so
  HTTP does not open;
- `/ready` returns `initializing`: Storage bucket initialization has not
  succeeded yet;
- 401: missing, invalid or expired API key (`WWW-Authenticate: Bearer`);
- 403 `insufficient_scope`: the key is valid but lacks the required scope or
  site access (response includes the missing `scope` and, when relevant, `site`);
- 405: known API path with an unsupported method; the `Allow` header lists the
  supported methods (OPTIONS returns 204 with the same header);
- 409 on `DELETE /v1/content/{item}/published`: the item is not published;
- 422 on Markdown upload: invalid frontmatter, slug, locale or YAML;
- 422 on public writes: invalid captcha, missing fields or invalid email. The
  most common cause is a **half-configured Turnstile**: the server has a global
  `CONTENTKIT_TURNSTILE_SECRET` but the target site has no `settings.turnstile_site_key`,
  so the rendered form has no widget, sends no token, and every submission fails
  `captcha verification failed`. Fix: set the site's public `turnstile_site_key`
  to the key that pairs with the secret (or use the Cloudflare test pair);
- an invisible/broken submit button on a form page: `settings.accent` was stored
  as a raw hex but reached `--primary` unconverted, so `hsl(var(--primary))`
  became invalid. The site builder converts hex→`H S% L%`; ensure `accent` is a
  hex or an HSL triple, then rebuild a release;
- contact/comment submissions succeed but no downstream notification arrives, or
  a per-submission `webhook delivery failed: endpoint returned 404`: the legacy
  built-in `CONTENTKIT_WEBHOOK_URL` (or a registered endpoint) points at an
  ingress path that does not exist on the receiver. Point it at a live hook and
  ensure the shared secret matches on both sides (the whole `whsec_...` string is
  the HMAC key verbatim, not base64-decoded);
- preview returns 404: token expired, was revoked or was typed incorrectly;
- custom-domain route returns 503: the site has no active release;
- every custom-domain path returns 404 (`{"error":"not found"}`): the request
  `Host` matches no site domain. A wildcard domain (`*.example.com`) matches
  sub-domains only, never the bare apex (`example.com`), so register the apex as
  its own domain (or serve it via `www.`) to avoid whole-site 404s;
- release request fails: render or storage upload failed; the previous active
  release remains active and a failure event is queued.
